title: Mossy Hollow
aesthetic: 14x14 cozy woodland critters, warm autumn palette, no armour in sight
_comment: A second sprite demo, deliberately NOT a re-skin of tiny-knight's armoured hero: round animal silhouettes instead of a humanoid rig, a soft brown outline instead of near-black, an autumn palette instead of steel-and-gold. Same bible mechanics (palette + reusable motifs), a totally different world — proving the artbook, not the engine, is what makes a set feel like a place. The leitmotif move is still a palette swap (fox-night); the reused-shape move is the 'leaf' charm, worn on the fox and enlarged on the signpost.

fox._comment: The hero's pose, stated in full once (like a soundtrack's main theme): a sitting fox, round ears with pink insides, a cream chest, a curled tail with a cream tip. Drawn without the outer outline — the engine's auto-outline traces the silhouette.

leaf._comment: A small leaf charm — the repeated SHAPE motif. Worn on the fox's chest and reappearing, enlarged, on the signpost: the same shape with two faces, the way a leitmotif recurs across cues.
